seoTitle: Port Wakefield vs Port Willunga — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Wakefield and Port Willunga. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Wakefield vs Port Willunga: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Wakefield and Port Willunga.
heading: Port Wakefield vs Port Willunga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Wakefield, South Australia and Port Willunga, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Willunga has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Port Wakefield (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Wakefield or Port Willunga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Wakefield has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Port Willunga scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Wakefield scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Port Willunga scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Wakefield or Port Willunga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Willunga has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
